name: Parque de las Presillas – circular from Alcorcón via the Butarque Stream bed
name_original: Parque de las Presillas – circular desde Alcorcón por el cauce del Arroyo Butarque
region_intro: Hiking trails around Alcorcón offer diverse outdoor experiences through its numerous parks and green spaces. The region is characterized by accessible paths, urban green areas, and natural features like the Butarque Stream and various lagoons. These areas provide a network of routes suitable for different fitness levels, from easy walks to more moderate trails. Best hiking trails around Alcorcón The most popular hiking route is Parque de las Presillas – circular from Alcorcón via the Butarque Stream bed, a 3.6 miles (5.9 km) trail that takes 1 hour 32 minutes to complete. This route offers an easy walk along the Butarque Stream. Another top favourite among local hikers is Presillas Park – Las Presillas Park loop from Joaquín Vilumbrales, a moderate 5.7 miles (9.2 km) path. This trail explores the varied terrain within Presillas Park. Local hikers also love the Presillas Park – Lake in Butarque Park loop from Joaquín Vilumbrales, a 7.1 miles (11.4 km) trail leading through green spaces and past the lake in Butarque Park, often completed in about 2 hours 59 minutes. Hiking around Alcorcón is defined by urban parks, green corridors, and accessible paths around lagoons. The network offers options for different ability levels, from leisurely strolls to longer, more active walks. The routes in Alcorcón are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 130 reviews. More than 1300 hikers have used komoot to explore Alcorcón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Alcorcón?
A: Alcorcón offers a wide selection of hiking opportunities, with over 40 routes available. These range from easy strolls to more moderate paths, ensuring options for various fitness levels.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Alcorcón?
A: Yes, Alcorcón has 16 easy hiking trails perfect for beginners or families. Many parks, like Parque de las Presillas, feature accessible paths. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e957221726/parque-de-las-presillas-circular-from-alcorcon-via-the-butarque-stream-bed" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque de las Presillas – circular from Alcorcón via the Butarque Stream bed&lt;/a&gt; is an easy, circular route ideal for a relaxed outing.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Alcorcón?
A: Absolutely. Many of Alcorcón's trails are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23654736" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Presillas Park – Las Presillas Park loop from Joaquín Vilumbrales&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate loop, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40361142" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de María Pascuala – María Pascuala Lagoon loop from Joaquín Vilumbrales&lt;/a&gt; offers a pleasant circuit around the lagoon.

Q: Are Alcorcón's hiking trails dog-friendly?
A: Many of Alcorcón's green spaces and parks are dog-friendly, making them suitable for walks with your canine companion. Parque la República, for instance, is noted for being welcoming to dogs. Always ensure your dog is on a leash and follow local regulations.

Q: What kind of natural features or attractions can I expect to see on hikes in Alcorcón?
A: Hiking in Alcorcón often takes you through urban parks and green corridors, featuring natural elements like the Butarque Stream and various lagoons. You might encounter the beautiful wooden bridge at Lago de Valderas or the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1499152" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake in Butarque Park&lt;/a&gt;. The area is known for its leafy vegetation, especially after rains.

Q: Are there any specific parks recommended for hiking in Alcorcón?
A: Parque de las Presillas is a key destination, offering a circular route along the Butarque Stream and a lake within Parque de Butarque. Other notable green spaces include Laguna de María Pascuala, Lago de Valderas, and Parque la República, all providing pleasant walking experiences.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Alcorcón?
A: The komoot community highly rates the trails in Alcorcón, giving them an average score of 4.6 stars from over 130 reviews. Hikers often praise the accessibility of the paths and the pleasant green environments for outdoor recreation.

Q: Are there any lakes or water features to explore on the trails?
A: Yes, Alcorcón's trails feature several beautiful water bodies. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1499152" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake in Butarque Park&lt;/a&gt;, walk around the Laguna de María Pascuala, or enjoy the pleasant path across Lago de Valderas with its wooden bridge. These spots are particularly scenic.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Alcorcón?
A: Alcorcón's parks and green spaces are enjoyable year-round. After rains, the vegetation becomes particularly lush and green, enhancing the natural beauty of the trails. Spring and autumn generally offer the most comfortable temperatures for hiking.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or castles near Alcorcón that can be visited after a hike?
A: While Alcorcón itself is known for its green spaces, the wider region offers historical sites. You could consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2865476" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Villaviciosa de Odón&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5599859" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torreón de Arroyomolinos&lt;/a&gt;, which are within a reasonable distance and offer a glimpse into the area's history.

Q: How long do hikes in Alcorcón typically take?
A: Hikes in Alcorcón vary in length and duration. Easy routes, such as the circular path in Parque de las Presillas, can take around 1.5 hours. Moderate trails, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33746405" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Presillas Park – Lake in Butarque Park loop from Joaquín Vilumbrales&lt;/a&gt;, might take closer to 3 hours, depending on your pace.
tips: Las Presillas Park offers a nice escape to spend a day with the whole family. Very close to the urban center of Alcorcón, inside the park you can find tables and benches, trails of easy difficulty, one of them that runs along the bed of the Butarque stream. A very picturesque place to connect with nature.
Walk on the way to Alcorcón park next to a stream when it has very pleasant and very flat water
